name: Socialbu Auto-Post & Return Links to GHL

parameters.subject: Error in download_image node Socialbu + GHL n8n workflow
parameters.message:
=Error message: Invalid URL: . URL must start with "http" or "https".

This error was caused because no image was uploaded by {{ $('Edit Fields').item.json.body.customData.contactId }} from the GHL level form

parameters.content:
# 🌐 Webhook – SocialBu Post Trigger
## This node receives data from an external system using a webhook.

In simple words:

Listens for POST requests at the socialbu_post endpoint

Receives JSON data from GoHighLevel (GHL)

Accepts form submissions, contact details, and custom content

Uses a shared secret (x-ghl-signature) for basic verification

Instantly triggers the workflow when data is received

### 👉 In short:
### This node acts as the entry point — it catches incoming data from GHL and starts the automation for SocialBu posting.

# 🔐 If – Signature Validation
## This node verifies the request is coming from a trusted source.

Checks the x-ghl-signature header

Compares it with the shared secret

Allows the workflow to continue only if it matches

### 👉 In short:
### This node protects the workflow from unauthorized requests.

# ✏️ Edit Fields – Prepare SocialBu Payload
## This node extracts and formats the required data.

Pulls post text from customData.text

Pulls image URL from customData.image

Stores the contact ID for tracking

Sets a callback URL for SocialBu responses

### 👉 In short:
### This node cleans and structures data before sending it to SocialBu.

# ⛔ Do Nothing – Invalid Request Path
## This node handles failed validation cases.

Runs when the signature check fails

Prevents any further processing

### 👉 In short:
### This node safely stops the workflow if the request is not trusted.

# 🧠 content_body
## This node separates the content into Blog and X (Twitter).

In simple words:

Takes one big text message

Finds the Blog Post part

Finds the X Tweet part

Splits them into two clean sections

If something is missing, it marks it as an error

### 👉 In short:
### This node turns one combined message into ready-to-post content for Blog and X.

# 🚦 Limit
## This node allows only one item to move forward.

In simple words:

Stops duplicate runs

Prevents posting the same thing again

Keeps the workflow clean and safe

### 👉 In short:
### This node makes sure the workflow runs only once.

# 🖼️ download_image
## This node downloads the image from a link.

In simple words:

Takes the image URL

Downloads the image automatically

If the image fails, the workflow still continues

Allows text-only posting if needed

### 👉 In short:
### This node safely fetches the image without breaking the automation.

# 🚀 initiate_upload
## This node starts the image upload process to SocialBu.

In simple words:

Sends the image name to SocialBu

Tells SocialBu what type of file it is (image)

Gets permission to upload the image

Prepares everything before the actual upload

### 👉 In short:
### This node tells SocialBu “Hey, an image is coming. Get ready.”

# ⚠️ no_image
## This node sends an alert when no image is found.

In simple words:

Checks if the image link is missing or broken

Sends an email notification

Explains why the image was not uploaded

Helps you know something went wrong

### 👉 In short:
### This node warns you when someone submits the form without an image.

# 🔀 pass_image_binary
## This node joins image data together.

In simple words:

Takes the image file

Combines it with upload details

Makes sure nothing is missing

Keeps image and info in one place

### 👉 In short:
### This node passes the image safely to the next step.

# 🖼️ upload_img
## This node uploads the actual image file.

In simple words:

Takes the image file from the workflow

Uploads it using a secure upload link

Sends the image exactly as required

Works even if the next step fails

### 👉 In short:
### This node physically uploads the image to SocialBu.

# 📡 status
## This node checks if the image upload was successful.

In simple words:

Asks SocialBu for upload status

Checks whether the image is ready to use

Confirms the upload finished properly

Prevents using a broken or incomplete image

### 👉 In short:
### This node confirms the image was uploaded correctly and is ready to post.

# ✅ uploaded
## This node checks if the image upload is complete.

In simple words:

Looks at the response message

Checks if it contains the word “uploaded”

Decides whether the upload finished successfully

Controls the next step based on upload status

### 👉 In short:
### This node confirms whether the image upload is done or not.

# ⏳ Wait
## This node pauses the workflow if upload is not ready.

In simple words:

Waits for 60 seconds

Gives SocialBu time to finish processing the image

Prevents moving forward too early

Used only when upload is still in progress

### 👉 In short:
### This node adds a delay so the system can finish uploading.

# 🔑 file_token
## This node saves the upload token for later use.

In simple words:

Extracts the upload_token from the response

Stores it in a clean field

Makes the token available for posting the image

Ensures the correct file reference is used

### 👉 In short:
### This node stores the uploaded image’s token for the next steps.

# 🔍 post_link
## This node checks whether the Facebook post link is available.

In simple words:

Looks at the permalink field in the response
Checks if the permalink is empty or null
Determines whether the post is fully published
Decides if the workflow should wait or retry

### 👉 In short:
### This node verifies if the Facebook post link has been generated.

# 📤 post_2_facebook
## This node creates a Facebook post using SocialBu API.

In simple words:

Sends a POST request to SocialBu
Publishes content to the selected Facebook account
Attaches the uploaded image using upload_token
Schedules the post 30 seconds ahead (New York time)
Controls privacy and comment settings
Sets a postback URL for status updates

### 👉 In short:
###This node publishes the Facebook post with text and image.

# 📊 post_status
## This node checks the status of the published post.

In simple words:

Fetches post details using the post ID
Retrieves the permalink and publish status
Confirms whether the post is live
Feeds data back into the workflow

### 👉 In short:
### This node confirms if the Facebook post is successfully published.

# ⏱️ 20_secs
## This node adds a short delay before checking post status.

In simple words:

Waits for 20 seconds
Gives SocialBu time to generate the post link
Prevents early status checks
Improves reliability of permalink fetching

### 👉 In short:
### This node waits briefly before rechecking post status.
# ⏳ Wait2
## This node adds a longer retry delay if the post link is still missing.

In simple words:

Waits for 10 seconds
Used when permalink is still not ready
Allows retry without failing the workflow
Helps handle slow API responses

### 👉 In short:
### This node delays the workflow to retry fetching the post link.

# 🔀 Merge
## This node combines post results from multiple platforms.

In simple words:

Collects results from Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter, and Google
Brings all post responses into one place
Makes sure nothing is missed
Prepares the data for clean processing

### 👉 In short:
### This node gathers all social post results together.

# ✂️ Split_urls
## This node separates each post link one by one.

In simple words:

Takes multiple permalink URLs
Splits them into individual items
Makes each link easier to identify
Prepares links for platform detection

### 👉 In short:
### This node breaks all post links into single URLs.

# 🧠 post_urls
## This node identifies which link belongs to which platform.

In simple words:

Reads all post URLs
Detects platform based on the link
Assigns links to Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter, and Google
Keeps the contact ID linked to the post
Creates one clean result

### 👉 In short:
### This node organizes post links by platform.

# 🚀 sendLink2_GHL
## This node sends the published post links to GoHighLevel.

In simple words:

Sends a message saying “Your success story has been published!”
Includes all social media post links
Connects the message to the correct contact
Uses a webhook to update GoHighLevel automatically

### 👉 In short:
### This node notifies the contact with all their published post links.

customData.text:
**BLOG POST:**

At FYZICAL Therapy and Balance Center Coral Springs, we celebrate the inspiring journey of James Sargent. Facing challenges with shoulder mobility and persistent back pain, James sought a path to improved health and vitality. The limitations in mobility were a significant hurdle, impacting daily activities and overall well-being.

Through a dedicated physical therapy program, James focused on alleviating pain associated with movement. The commitment to therapy sessions and exercises tailored to individual needs played a crucial role in this transformative journey. The goal was clear: to remove pain and enhance mobility, allowing for a more active lifestyle.

The results have been remarkable. James now enjoys increased activity levels, experiencing the freedom that comes with reduced pain and improved motion. This success story underscores the power of personalized physical therapy in overcoming physical challenges and enhancing quality of life.

At FYZICAL Therapy and Balance Center Coral Springs, we are proud to support patients like James in their pursuit of health and wellness. #PhysicalTherapy #RecoveryJourney #CoralSpringsPT

**X tweet:**

James Sargent's journey at FYZICAL Coral Springs highlights the power of physical therapy. Overcoming shoulder and back pain, James now enjoys improved mobility and an active lifestyle. A true testament to personalized care. #PhysicalTherapy #CoralSpringsPT
